accessibility: All interactive elements MUST be reachable and activatable by keyboard. No interaction pathway may be mouse-only.
All modal dialogs MUST trap focus while open. Focus MUST return to the triggering element on modal close.
Destructive confirmation dialogs must have role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and focus trapped.
All input fields must have associated <label> elements and error messages use aria-describedby.
Global keyboard shortcuts must not conflict with browser or assistive technology defaults. Shortcuts MUST NOT activate when focus is inside an <input>, <textarea>, or <select>.
Escape closes any open modal, panel, context menu, or dropdown that does not contain a pending constitutional confirmation. For constitutional confirmation modals, Escape is disabled.
All ARIA attributes, live regions, and focus management requirements apply to all rooms without exception as a constitutional floor.
Form inputs must use a minimum font-size of 16px on mobile to prevent automatic zoom on iOS Safari.
Room level empty state animations must be suppressed when actor.prefers_reduced_motion == 'reduce'.
Illustrations must not be shown at mobile breakpoint in empty states.

workflows.description: Permanent deletion of any Container, relation, file, or provenance record is constitutionally prohibited. The `permanent: true` flag on any deletion API call MUST be rejected. This rule has no exceptions and no authority level that can override it.

Files uploaded to Organyz are immutable and non-deletable from storage. The UI must never offer a "Delete file" action that purges a file from storage or audit history. Logical detachment of a file from a Container is permitted; physical deletion is never exposed.

Provenance records, history entries, and audit trail entries are permanently preserved regardless of the lifecycle state of the Container they reference. Deleting a Container does not delete its history.
Components must interpolate gracefully between breakpoints. No hard pixel assumptions about device type (phone vs. laptop) are permitted.
All layout spacing must use values from the Design System (v2.0) spacing table. Arbitrary pixel values not present in that table are constitutionally inadmissible.
Workflow state badges are always visible. No layout adaptation may hide, clip, or obscure a Container's workflow state badge.
Conflict indicators and blocker indicators are always visible. Responsive layout must not collapse these into hidden overflow at any breakpoint.
The (+ ADD NEW) instrument is always reachable without scrolling. Its position changes by breakpoint (see Section 4.4) but it is never inside a scroll container.

Room identity is always clear. At every breakpoint, the user must be able to identify which room they are in without ambiguity. The active room must be indicated in the sidebar (desktop/tablet) or topbar (mobile) at all times.

Responsive layout changes must not alter constitutional room permissions. A FIND room rendered at mobile breakpoint is still read-only. A HOME room rendered at tablet breakpoint still prohibits mutations. Responsive adaptation never grants or removes authority.
Layout changes must not cause data loss. Collapsing a panel, switching breakpoints, or resizing the viewport must not discard unsaved form data, editor content, or filter state.
Form inputs must use a minimum font-size of 16px on mobile to prevent automatic zoom on iOS Safari. This overrides any other type scale specification for input fields at the mobile breakpoint only.

All ARIA attributes, live regions, and focus management requirements defined in this document and in Accessibility Interactions (v1.0) apply to all rooms without exception. Accessibility compliance is not a feature — it is a constitutional floor.
The action menu (⋮) MUST be the access point for all Tertiary actions. It MUST appear on card hover (Secondary disclosure) and is always available via keyboard navigation.
A 300ms click debounce MUST be applied to all actionable elements to prevent accidental double-activation. This does not apply to text inputs.
Adds constitutional rules governing the ambient layer's behavior, scope limits, and labeling obligations.
Admit Drafts to Canonical
AI Assist panel entry animation
The AI Coherence Sidebar updates its content based on user writing.
steps: User completes a paragraph (Enter key or focus departure)
Sidebar updates with new coherence items within 2 seconds

workflows.description: AI Conversation Models

When a Draft is sent to DESK, AI provenance is preserved. Segments tagged as AI-generated in STUDIO transfer their AI origin metadata to the Container record. The Container in DESK carries the AI badge on its card. Subsequent edits by the user in DESK do not remove the AI badge from the Container-level record; they add to the provenance chain.
User clicks an AI action, panel shows loading state, then a review modal opens.
steps: Panel shows loading state: "✨ Working…" with spinner

workflows.description: System-initiated process by the Ambient Layer to analyze already-admitted material and surface findings.
Appendix B Canonical Entity Register Master
Sets lifecycle_state = Archived. The Container is treated as inactive and deprioritized but is more explicitly accessible than a Deleted Container.
Archive and logically delete Containers per Deletion Constitution v1.0
Archive uses an inline confirmation pattern, less disruptive and easily reversible.
Artifact Bundle Inspection Guide
Async operation completion causes the element to exit Loading state and provides outcome feedback.
Async operation failure causes the element to exit Loading state and provides error feedback.
Async operation initiation causes the element to enter a Loading state.

Auto-save fires every 10 seconds while typing is active and immediately on editor blur. Draft state is persisted to backend on every save. Local crash recovery: the editor holds a local-storage backup updated every 5 seconds as a fallback.
Meaning with real-world consequence, immutable, creates active obligation, visible only in DESK.
Container enters this state when a declared dependency is unfulfilled, suspending certain transitions.
Blocked transition (Blocked condition) produces a gate rejection with a blocking Container link.
Bulk deletion requires a modal that aggregates items, shows individual names, and includes specific consequence statements and interlocks for Binding-state Containers.
Canonical Canvases Conversation Commit
Canonical Claim Eligibility Gate
Canonical Freeze And Closure Conditions
Authoritative meaning, reviewed and admitted, visible, immutable content (edits require new Draft).
Canvas Promotion Rule
Card drag to create a relation between two Containers in DESK (opens relation type modal).
Claim Class Taxonomy Identity State Event Derived
Maintains a per-project coherence score reflecting unresolved contradictions, gaps, and drift.
Terminal state, purpose fulfilled, preserved permanently, no active obligation.
Compliance Harness Specification
Tracks how the meaning or framing of concepts shifts across documents over time.

All SETTINGS changes persist to the backend immediately on save. There is no batch-save across panels. Settings do not use localStorage for any configuration data (though non-critical UI preferences like last-active tab may be cached locally).
Conflict Objects And Resolution Workflow
Requires modal with side-by-side comparison for conflict resolution.
Container enters this state when a declared conflict exists on its content, suspending certain transitions.
Requires confirmation modal naming the connector explicitly for connector disconnect.
Connector Models

Connector status (connected/disconnected) is fetched live on SETTINGS open and on each tab visit. Connection state is never assumed from cache — OAuth revocation by the external service may change it at any time.
Constitutional Compiler Requirements And Interfaces Specification

All constitutional operations (workflow transitions, conflict resolution, ingestion confirmation) MUST show a confirmation modal before execution. No constitutional operation executes on a single click without a modal gate.
A single click on a Container card opens the detail view. It does not execute any workflow operation.

Initial canonical definition of the Container Ingestion Constitution. Establishes the Ingestion Axiom, the System-Resolved semanticType Law, the Draft-First Guarantee, legal ingestion channels, trait signal categories, the recognition pipeline, SemanticType Trait Registry governance, Unresolved Container handling, and ingestion provenance requirements.
Defines the states and valid transitions for Containers within the Organyz system.

Full rewrite. Supersedes v1's glass-bg token references and Tailwind color names with exact Design System v2 token bindings throughout. Establishes the eight-zone card anatomy with mandatory/optional rows. Re-specifies all five workflow state badges with DS v2 surface colors (replacing generic gray/blue/green/dark-green/red with exact rgba and var() values). Adds complete overlay system: semanticType badge with 13-type icon/color table bound to DS v2, StatusContent badge (read-only, provenance tooltip), AI badge (lavender, always visible), conflict badge (rose floating chip), blocker badge (gold floating chip), Unresolved state (gold pulse). Per-room variants for DESK (checkbox, priority accent, deadline), FIND (search highlight, read-only indicator, prohibited actions), STUDIO (compact mode, active state, auto-save indicator), HOME (severity accent, source room label, navigation-only rule). Card entry animation (fadeUp 400ms, index × 40ms stagger). Size/density table with minimum heights. Twelve constitutional rules (CC-001 through CC-012).
Ingestion channel CH-03: Content paste into STUDIO editor.
Maintains the state of reasoning for each project so it can be reconstructed on demand.
Identifies Containers whose claims conflict with each other.
steps: Conflict Resolution Modal shows full evidence chain

workflows.description: Describes the propagation of contradiction findings from the Ambient Layer to FIND, HOME, and DESK.
steps: Ambient Layer detects contradiction between Container A and Container B
FIND: Knowledge Health Indicator (⚠ Conflict chip) appears on both result cards when they appear in any FIND result set. AI Connection Surface in Detail Panel shows the contradiction.
HOME: Attention Register gains a new "Conflict" item (rose, high priority) per HOME Room Specification v2.0 H-006. Item names the destination room: "View in DESK →"

DESK: Conflict record is registered in the Integrity Overlay. Both Containers are flagged. Resolution requires explicit human action per Workflow State Constitution v2.0 and DESK Room Specification v2.0 D-014.

workflows.description: Core Structural Models
Cross Document Consistency Audit V 1 Build Contract Schemas Apis
Destructive confirmation dialogs must have role="alertdialog" with aria-modal="true" and focus trapped.
Declare Conflicts on Containers
Defines the complete set of AI interaction modes, their authority, and relationships.
Formally defines the Ambient AI Layer as the background analysis capability.
Defines how AI-surfaced findings propagate across rooms to establish system-level coherence.
Degraded Mode Requirements
Containers may declare dependencies on other Containers, meaning progression is contingent on completion of the named Container.
Requires inline confirmation naming the blocking Container for dependency removal.
When a blocking Container completes, dependent Containers are evaluated and potentially unblocked.
steps: System evaluates 'blocks' connectors referencing the completed Container.
For each dependent Container in 'Blocked' state, system checks if all dependencies are fulfilled.
If all dependencies are fulfilled, 'Blocked' condition is cleared automatically.
System emits a provenance record on each unblocked Container.
Owner of each unblocked Container receives a notification.

workflows.description: Removes a directional link between two Containers (a relation) or removes a file attachment from a Container (an AttachmentContent link). The detached Container and the detached file are not affected — only the link is removed.
Detach uses an inline confirmation pattern, naming the specific item being detached.
Display results, previews, and traceable summaries grounded in admissible sources

If [Delete] is chosen, Draft lifecycle_state is set to Deleted

workflows.description: Every Container produced by an ingestion event enters the system in WorkflowState: Draft.
Initial state of a Container, non-binding meaning, editable, no obligation.

Drag and drop within a list or grid is permitted only when all of the following are true: items are in the same workflow state, items are in the same room, and items belong to the same semantic collection. Reordering updates `display_order` metadata only and MUST NOT mutate workflow state, semanticType, or any semantic relationship.
Entry Identity Law

Escape closes any open modal, panel, context menu, or dropdown that does not contain a pending constitutional confirmation. For constitutional confirmation modals, Escape is disabled — the user must click [Cancel] explicitly.
Evidence Strength Advisory Only Scoped By Container
Execute queries explicitly initiated by the user
Execute workflow transitions: Draft → Canonical → Binding → Completed
Failed workflow transition (gate rejection) produces a rejection message in modal with specific failed condition.
Federation External Models

When a file is dragged from the desktop into the application window, an overlay appears, and upon drop, the ingestion pipeline initiates.

workflows.description: Updating a file creates a new AttachmentContent superseding the old, rather than in-place editing or overwriting.
Filter, sort, and rank results by user-selected criteria
A FIND handoff to DESK MUST navigate to DESK with the Container pre-selected and the detail panel open. The user arrives in DESK with full context and can immediately act on the Container.
A FIND handoff to STUDIO MUST navigate to STUDIO with the Container loaded in the editor canvas as the starting content for a new Draft. STUDIO creates a new Draft; FIND does not create it.
All input fields must have associated <label> elements and error messages use aria-describedby.
Each SETTINGS panel maintains its own unsaved-change state. Changes in one panel do not affect others.
Form validation error produces an inline error below the field.
Generation Depth And Epistemic Distance
Global keyboard shortcuts must be implemented and not conflict with browser or assistive technology defaults. Shortcuts MUST NOT activate when focus is inside an <input>, <textarea>, or <select>.
Handoffs MUST NOT auto-execute any mutation in the destination room. The user lands in the destination room with context pre-loaded but must initiate any action explicitly.
Sequence of events for a Handoff operation.
steps: SYSTEM: Confirmation: "Send [Container title] to [ROOM]?" — inline confirm, not a full modal
SYSTEM: Navigation transitions to destination room (150ms fade out / 150ms fade in)
SYSTEM: Destination room loads with the handed-off Container: Animated yellow border flash, Container scrolled into view if not visible, "Handed off from [SOURCE ROOM]" label fades after 3 seconds

workflows.description: Human-initiated process to capture new material and admit it to the knowledge base.
Every piece of content that enters Organyz becomes a Container, wrapped with at minimum one Semantic Entity and one semanticType assignment.
User selects one or more files through the OS file picker or a designated upload surface. The system receives raw binary content and initiates the recognition pipeline.
User drags content from an external source (OS desktop, browser, another application) onto a legal drop target within the Organyz UI. Constitutionally equivalent to CH-01 after boundary acceptance.

User pastes content from the system clipboard into a legal paste target. Content may be plain text, rich text, or binary. The system extracts the highest-fidelity representation available from the clipboard payload.

User explicitly creates content through a structured form surface (e.g., the (+ ADD NEW) instrument). The user provides structured input; the system wraps it in a Container. semanticType is pre-declared by the form's jurisdiction context and confirmed by the recognition pipeline.

Content arrives from an authorized external connector (e.g., Google Drive, GitHub). The connector provides content payload and source metadata. The system applies the full recognition pipeline regardless of source system classification.
Content is submitted programmatically via the Organyz API by an authorized service or integration. The system applies the full recognition pipeline to all API-pushed content.
Requires inline confirmation on Provisional Container for ingestion semanticType commitment.
Initiate conflict resolution
Inspect Container provenance, relations, and version history (read-only)
Terminal state, content declared void, preserved permanently with reason, no authority or obligation.
Requires confirmation modal with explicit reason for any irreversible deletion.

Italic in Fraunces is always semantic — it signals the core product value proposition. One italic phrase per headline, always at the end, always in a brand color (emerald, blue, or gold depending on context).
All interactive elements MUST be reachable and activatable by keyboard. No interaction pathway may be mouse-only.

Sets lifecycle_state = Deleted on the Container. The Container ceases to appear in normal room views, search results, and list surfaces. It is retained in the system with its full history, relations, and content intact.
Logical delete requires a full confirmation modal with specific content and non-dismissible behavior.
Minimum Signal Thresholds For Promotion
All modal dialogs MUST trap focus while open. Focus MUST return to the triggering element on modal close.
Mode Governance Law
Object Schemas V 1 Containers Connectors Events
Every interaction that produces a state change MUST produce at minimum one observable feedback signal (toast, badge update, or inline indicator). Silent state changes are constitutionally prohibited.
Organyz Attribute Register
Organyz Backend Spec Provider Accounts Credentials And User Grants
Organyz Locale Pack En Customer
Organyz Locale Pack Es Customer
Perform handoffs — sending a Container reference to DESK or STUDIO
Perform handoffs to STUDIO (sends a Container reference, not a state change)
Persist user preferences (saved searches, view mode, filter history) as non-canonical preference data
System assembles full context automatically before a decision is committed.
steps: User prepares to admit a Container
Admission Confirmation Modal displays Context Summary section

workflows.description: Scroll position MUST be preserved when the user navigates between rooms and returns. A room that resets scroll position on revisit loses session continuity, which is constitutionally prohibited.

An action that contradicts a room's constitutional purpose MUST NOT be rendered in that room — not even in a disabled state. Disabled actions imply availability; prohibited actions must be absent entirely.

No animation or transition may prevent the user from taking their next action. All micro-interactions must complete passively while the interface remains interactive. Blocking animations are constitutionally prohibited.
Dragging a Container between rooms is prohibited. Cross-room movement requires explicit handoff actions.
Dragging a Container between workflow states is prohibited. Moving a Draft to a Canonical list by drag is constitutionally invalid. State transitions require explicit workflow operations.
Optimistic UI updates are prohibited for workflow state transitions. The UI MUST wait for server confirmation before updating the workflow state badge or card treatment.
Promotion Demotion And Canonical Liability Events

All queries must be explicitly initiated by the user. Incremental retrieval (live search-as-you-type suggestions) is permitted within an active FIND session. Background or ambient querying outside the session is prohibited.
Ordered sequence of operations from ingestion event to Container production.
When a Container card is dragged onto another Container card in DESK, a relation modal opens.
steps: relation modal opens: "Create relation between [A] and [B]?"
relation type selector: depends-on / blocks / related-to / references
on Create: dashed connector animates between cards, success toast

workflows.description: Continuously maps semantic connections between Containers.
Renderer Adapter Layer Spec V 1 Deterministic Render Execution
Renderer Resolution Engine
Restores a Deleted or Archived Container to an Active lifecycle_state.
steps: Trigger: [Restore] button on the suppressed card
On confirmation: lifecycle_state is set to Active
Container immediately reappears in normal room views

workflows.description: Retrieve across verticals when explicitly scoped by the user
Rework operation creates a new Draft Container from a Canonical Container, copying data and establishing a rework_of connector, leaving the original Canonical unchanged.
steps: Creates a new Container with a new container_id
Copies all entities and metadata from the Canonical source
Sets the new Container's workflow_state to Draft
Creates a rework_of connector from the new Draft to the Canonical source
The Canonical source remains Canonical and unchanged
The new Draft is the active working copy

workflows.description: Create and Admit a Task: Draft → Canonical
User creates a Task directly in DESK and admits it immediately.
steps: USER: Selects "Task", enters details, selects destination: DESK.
SYSTEM: Creates Container (semanticType: Task, WorkflowState: Draft) in DESK.
SYSTEM: Success toast: "Task created in DESK"
SYSTEM: Opens Admission Confirmation Modal (same as S-01).
GATE: Evaluates Admission Gate. All conditions pass.
SYSTEM: Success toast: "Task admitted to Canonical"

workflows.description: Admit a Draft Directly in DESK: Draft → Canonical
Bind a Canonical Task: Canonical → Binding
User marks a Binding Task as Completed, fulfilling the obligation and unblocking any dependent Containers.
steps: USER: Clicks [Complete] on a Binding Task card in DESK.
SYSTEM: Opens the Completion Confirmation Modal.
SYSTEM: Displays: Container title, current [Binding] badge → [Completed] badge
SYSTEM: Warning: "This action is permanent. The Task will be closed and its obligation fulfilled."
SYSTEM: Optional: Completion notes field (free text, optional).
SYSTEM: Validation checklist: ✓ You have completion authority, ✓ Task is in Binding state, ✓ No blocking integrity violations
GATE: Evaluates Completion Gate (Section 6.3 of Workflow State Constitution).
SYSTEM: Records provenance (action: completed, actor, timestamp, notes if provided).

SYSTEM: Evaluates dependency graph: Finds all Containers declaring a dependency on this Container, For each dependent Container where all dependencies are now fulfilled: Clears the Blocked conditional state, Records unblocking provenance on the dependent Container, Emits notification to the dependent Container's owner
SYSTEM: Card visual treatment: muted, no active border.
SYSTEM: If any Containers were unblocked: Additional toast: "2 blocked items are now unblocked"

workflows.description: Complete a Canonical Document: Canonical → Completed
User discards a Draft that is no longer needed, preserving it in the historical record as Invalidated rather than deleting it.
steps: USER: Clicks [Invalidate] on a Draft card.
SYSTEM: Warning: "This action is permanent. The Draft will be preserved but marked as void."
SYSTEM: Reason field (required, minimum 10 characters): Placeholder: "Why is this Draft being invalidated?"
USER: Enters reason: e.g. "Superseded by revised approach agreed in meeting"
SYSTEM: Records provenance (action: invalidated, reason, actor, timestamp).
SYSTEM: Card visual treatment: muted, dashed border.

workflows.description: Invalidate a Canonical Container: Canonical → Invalidated
A Binding Container must be voided — e.g., a Contract becomes invalid due to changed circumstances. This is the most consequential invalidation and requires the highest confirmation bar.
steps: USER: Clicks [Invalidate] on a Binding card in DESK.
SYSTEM: Opens Binding Invalidation Confirmation Modal.
SYSTEM: Elevated warning: "This Container is Binding. Invalidating it voids an active obligation. This action is permanent and irreversible."
SYSTEM: Displays: All active obligations / actors named in the Container.
SYSTEM: Reason field (required, minimum 30 characters for Binding invalidation).
SYSTEM: Secondary confirmation: User must type the Container title to confirm.
GATE: Requires Container title confirmation string match.
USER: Types the Container title into the confirmation field.
SYSTEM: Records provenance (action: invalidated, elevated authority noted, reason, actor, timestamp).
SYSTEM: Dependent Containers in Blocked state due to this Container are NOT automatically unblocked (invalidation ≠ completion).
SYSTEM: Emits notification to all actors named in the Container.
SYSTEM: Notification to named actors: "A Binding item you were associated with has been invalidated."

workflows.description: Rework a Canonical Container: Canonical → new Draft
Resolve a Blocked Container: Blocked cleared
A Container is in the Blocked conditional state because a dependency has not been fulfilled. The blocking Container is completed, unblocking the dependent.
steps: SYSTEM: Container B transitions to Completed (actor performs S-04).
SYSTEM: Evaluates all Containers with a blocks connector referencing B.
SYSTEM: Finds Container A in Blocked conditional state.
SYSTEM: Verifies all of Container A's declared dependencies are now Completed.
SYSTEM: Clears Blocked condition on Container A.
SYSTEM: Records unblocking provenance on Container A: "Unblocked by completion of [Container B title] at [timestamp]"
SYSTEM: Emits notification to Container A's owner: "[Container B title] was completed. Your item [Container A title] is now unblocked."

workflows.description: A Container is in the Blocked conditional state because a dependency has not been fulfilled. The blocking Container is completed, unblocking the dependent. Manual resolution flow (if actor chooses to remove the dependency rather than waiting).
steps: USER: Opens Container A in DESK.
USER: Navigates to the Dependencies section.
USER: Clicks [Remove Dependency] next to the blocking Container B reference.
SYSTEM: Opens confirmation: "Remove dependency on [Container B title]? Container A will be unblocked."
SYSTEM: Clears Blocked condition on Container A.
SYSTEM: Records removal in provenance with actor identity.
SYSTEM: Toast: "Dependency removed. Item is unblocked."

workflows.description: Declare and Resolve a Conflict: Conflicted → resolved
The complete end-to-end journey of a Task Container through all states, crossing two rooms.
steps: USER: Creates Task in STUDIO via (+ ADD NEW).
USER: Authors the Task — defines scope, success criteria, assignees.
SYSTEM: Edits recorded in provenance. No versioning events.
SYSTEM: Handoff created. User navigated to DESK.
USER: Reviews the Task in DESK.
SYSTEM: Validation: ✓ authority ✓ Draft state ✓ no violations ✓ no Unresolved semanticType
SYSTEM: New Canonical Container created. Supersedes Draft.
USER: Assigns the Task to a team member.
USER: Clicks [Bind] to activate the obligation.
SYSTEM: Consequence: "Activating Binding creates an active obligation on the named actor to complete this work."
SYSTEM: Validation: ✓ authority ✓ Canonical ✓ no violations ✓ no conflicts ✓ not blocked
USER: Clicks [Change Status] → selects "Blocked" → domain "External" → reason "Waiting for vendor API credentials".
SYSTEM: New StatusContent entity created and attached.
SYSTEM: NOTE: This is a StatusContent update, not a workflow state change. WorkflowState remains Binding.
USER: Blocker resolves. User clicks [Change Status] → "In Progress".
SYSTEM: Validation: ✓ authority ✓ Binding state ✓ no blocking violations
USER: Enters notes: "Integration launched. All endpoints verified."
SYSTEM: Dependency graph evaluated: any Containers blocked by this Task are unblocked.
SYSTEM: Unblocked items toast (if any): "2 blocked items are now unblocked"

workflows.description: Full Lifecycle — Document from Draft to Completed: Draft → Canonical → Completed
Section D Core Semantic Constructs Canonical Register
